seo.title: AI Agent Observability in LlamaIndex Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in LlamaIndex agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Observability in LlamaIndex reveals critical failure modes in RAG pipelines where agents fail to retrieve relevant context, hallucinate beyond indexed documents, suffer from stale indexes, or lose context due to chunking errors. These issues manifest uniquely through LlamaIndex's query engines, retrievers, and synthesizers, leading to unreliable agent responses. Vex provides seamless observability by wrapping LlamaIndex query operations to detect and trace these failures in production.

howItManifests.symptom: QueryEngine returns VectorStoreIndex nodes with low similarity scores (<0.7) despite user query specificity
howItManifests.impact: Agent generates generic or wrong answers, eroding user trust in 40%+ of queries

howItManifests.symptom: ResponseSynthesisNode contains phrases not grounded in retrieved NodeWithScore content
howItManifests.impact: Legal/compliance violations from fabricated facts in 25% of enterprise queries

howItManifests.symptom: Document metadata.last_modified < 30 days ago but index lacks recent embeddings
howItManifests.impact: Critical business decisions based on 15%+ outdated product/pricing data

howItManifests.scenario: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
howItManifests.symptom: Critical sentences split across Node chunks, causing retriever to miss key context
howItManifests.impact: 30% drop in answer accuracy for technical documentation queries

detection.name: Retriever Relevance Score Monitor
detection.description: Detects irrelevant context by monitoring average NodeWithScore similarity scores from VectorStoreIndex queries
detection.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader

from llama_index.core.retrievers import VectorIndexRetriever

# During query execution
retriever = VectorStoreIndex.as_retriever(similarity_top_k=3)

nodes = retriever.retrieve('What is the latest pricing?')

avg_score = sum(node.score for node in nodes) / len(nodes)

if avg_score < 0.7:
    print(f'🚨 LOW RELEVANCE: avg similarity {avg_score:.2f}')
    # Log to Vex

print(f'Avg relevance score: {avg_score:.2f}')

detection.name: Hallucination Grounding Check
detection.description: Compares synthesized response against retrieved context using LlamaIndex's ResponseSynthesizer metadata

detection.name: Index Freshness Audit
detection.description: Checks document metadata timestamps against index embedding dates in SimpleDirectoryReader indexes

fixes.name: Hybrid Retriever with Keyword Fallback
fixes.description: Combines semantic search with BM25 keyword retriever to catch missed relevant chunks
fixes.code:
from llama_index.core.retrievers import BM25Retriever, VectorIndexRetriever
from llama_index.core.query_engine import RetrieverQueryEngine
from llama_index.core.postprocessor import SimilarityPostprocessor

vector_retriever = VectorStoreIndex.as_retriever(similarity_top_k=2)
bm25_retriever = BM25Retriever.from_defaults(nodes=index.docstore.docs.values(), similarity_top_k=2)

# Ensemble retriever
class HybridRetriever:
    def __init__(self, vector_retriever, bm25_retriever):
        self.vector_retriever = vector_retriever
        self.bm25_retriever = bm25_retriever
    
    def retrieve(self, query):
        vector_nodes = self.vector_retriever.retrieve(query)
        bm25_nodes = self.bm25_retriever.retrieve(query)
        return list(set(vector_nodes + bm25_nodes))[:4]

query_engine = RetrieverQueryEngine.from_args(HybridRetriever(vector_retriever, bm25_retriever))

fixes.name: Faithful Response Synthesizer
fixes.description: Forces LlamaIndex synthesizer to stay grounded using citation-only prompting
fixes.code:
from llama_index.core.prompts import PromptTemplate
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI

llm = OpenAI(model='gpt-4o-mini', temperature=0.1)

response_synth_prompt = PromptTemplate(
    "Context information is below.\n---------------------\n{context_str}\n---------------------\nAnswer the question based ONLY on the context above. "
    "If you cannot answer from context, say 'I don't have that information'.\n\nQuestion: {query_str}\nAnswer:"
)

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=5,
    response_mode='compact',
    llm=llm,
    response_synthesizer=ResponseSynthesizer.from_args(
        response_mode='simple_summarize',
        use_prompt=True,
        prompt=response_synth_prompt
    )
)

fixes.name: Overlap Chunking Strategy
fixes.description: Uses SentenceSplitter with overlap to prevent chunk boundary context loss
fixes.code:
from llama_index.core.node_parser import SentenceSplitter

node_parser = SentenceSplitter(
    chunk_size=512,
    chunk_overlap=100,  # 20% overlap
    paragraph_separator='\n\n',
    secondary_chunking_regex=r'[^\n\t]+'
)

# Rebuild index with overlap
service_context = ServiceContext.from_defaults(node_parser=node_parser)
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents, service_context=service_context)

print(f'✅ Fixed chunking with {node_parser.chunk_overlap} token overlap')

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically wraps LlamaIndex query_engine.query() calls to capture full trace context including NodeWithScore similarity scores, ResponseSynthesizer outputs, and document metadata. It detects all four failure modes using production-grade thresholds and provides one-click remediation code. Vex's LlamaIndex integration runs zero-config, capturing 100% of agent queries with <5ms overhead.

The platform surfaces issues through real-time dashboards showing relevance score distributions, hallucination rates by query type, index freshness SLAs, and chunking quality metrics. Automated alerts trigger at configurable thresholds (e.g., avg similarity <0.75) with root cause analysis linking back to specific index nodes and query patterns.

Remediation workflows auto-generate fixed query engines with hybrid retrievers, grounded synthesizers, and overlap chunking. Vex maintains golden datasets of verified queries to continuously validate agent reliability as data and models evolve.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader

# Initialize Vex (one-time)
vex.init(project='llama-agent-observability')

# Load your LlamaIndex setup
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('./data').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(similarity_top_k=3)

# Vex automatically observes ALL queries
@vex.span()
def query_with_observability(query: str):
    with vex.trace(name=f'llama-query-{hash(query) % 1000}'):
        response = query_engine.query(query)
        
        # Vex auto-captures:
        # - Node similarity scores
        # - Response vs source grounding
        # - Document timestamps
        # - Chunk boundaries
        
        vex.span.set_attribute('relevance.avg_score', sum(n.score for n in response.source_nodes) / len(response.source_nodes))
        vex.span.set_attribute('hallucination.risk', len(response.response) > 2 * sum(len(n.text) for n in response.source_nodes))
        
        return response

# Production usage
result = query_with_observability('What are current API rate limits?')
print(result)

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install with `pip install vex-sdk llama-index` and wrap your query_engine.query() calls to catch 100% of observability failures in production.
